edge_hypothesis: MACD tail harvester 7:1 on 4h is the new MACD champion (PF=1.712, DD=7.5%, 161 trades, ALL regimes profitable). MACD 6:1 on 1h already works (PF=1.460, 170 trades, ALL regimes profitable). Two critical questions: (1) DOES THE 7:1 PEAK REPLICATE ON 1H? On 4h, the MACD R:R curve is: 3.5:1→1.347, 5:1→1.353, 7:1→1.712, 8:1→1.442, 12:1→1.302. The 7:1 peak on 4h significantly outperforms 8:1 (by +0.27 PF). On 1h, only 6:1 has been tested (PF=1.460). If 7:1 on 1h outperforms 6:1 on 1h, the 7:1 peak is timeframe-portable — meaning the MACD histogram cross has a stable, template-intrinsic optimal R:R that doesn't shift with bar size. This would be a significant meta-finding about signal quality vs. R:R optimization. (2) DOES 6:1 WORK ON 4H? There's a gap in the 4h R:R data: 5:1→1.353, then directly to 7:1→1.712. The massive +0.36 PF jump between 5:1 and 7:1 on 4h is suspicious — 6:1 data would reveal whether the improvement is gradual (5:1→6:1→7:1 monotonic) or sudden (flat until 7:1 then a cliff). This shapes how we think about the R:R curve — gradual suggests broad optimum, sudden suggests fragile peak. Regime assumption: ALL (both MACD 7:1/4h and MACD 6:1/1h are all-regimes-profitable). Invalidation: (1) If 7:1 on 1h underperforms 6:1 on 1h, the R:R peak IS timeframe-dependent and each template+timeframe combo needs its own R:R optimization. (2) If 6:1 on 4h is close to 7:1 on 4h (PF > 1.6), the optimal R:R is a broad plateau — more robust. If 6:1 on 4h is close to 5:1 (PF ~1.35), the 7:1 peak is a sharp spike — fragile and possibly overfit.

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_7to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R (1.0x ATR stop / 7.0x ATR TP). The R:R peak portability test. MACD 7:1 on 4h = PF 1.712 (champion). MACD 6:1 on 1h = PF 1.460. If 7:1 on 1h > 1.460, the peak transfers across timeframes. The MACDh_12_26_9 histogram captures momentum acceleration — on 1h, this detects intra-day momentum shifts that can produce strong 4-8 hour continuation moves. 7.0x ATR on 1h targets these continuation legs. Breakeven win rate: 12.5%. Expected: ~170 trades (matching 6:1 trade count since entry signal is identical), PF range 1.3-1.8 depending on whether 7:1 is the universal MACD peak.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (histogram bullish zero-line cross).
Close > EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price above medium-term average).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (histogram bearish zero-line cross).
Close < EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price below medium-term average).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend confirmation: Close > EMA_50 (long), Close < EMA_50 (short)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
R:R peak portability test: is 7:1 the universal MACD peak across timeframes?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend confirmation on 1h — faster than 4h, captures intra-day momentum
MACDh_12_26_9 histogram zero-line cross — testing 7:1 peak portability to 1h
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — replicating 4h champion R:R on 1h timeframe

variants.name: macd_4h_tail_harvester_6to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/4h with 6:1 R:R (1.0x ATR stop / 6.0x ATR TP). Fills the gap in the 4h R:R curve between 5:1 (PF=1.353) and 7:1 (PF=1.712). The +0.36 PF jump between 5:1 and 7:1 is the largest single-step improvement in any R:R curve in the system. If 6:1 on 4h produces PF ~1.5 (midpoint), the improvement is gradual and the 7:1 peak is a broad plateau — robust. If 6:1 on 4h produces PF ~1.35 (close to 5:1), the improvement is sudden at 7:1 — a narrow peak that may be fragile/overfit. This single data point reshapes our confidence in the 7:1 peak. Note: 6:1 on 1h achieved PF=1.460, so the 4h version should have comparable or higher PF if the timeframe pattern holds. Breakeven win rate: 14.3%.

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_8to1_tight
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R (1.0x ATR stop / 8.0x ATR TP). On 4h, MACD declines from 7:1 peak (PF=1.712) to 8:1 (PF=1.442). On 1h, only 6:1 tested (PF=1.460). The 1h MACD 8:1 ultra was tested before but on a different data window — this retests with identical tight-stop parameters (1.0x ATR) to see if the 4h decline pattern (7:1→8:1) holds on 1h or if 1h has a different R:R shape. If 1h MACD performs BETTER at 8:1 than 7:1 (opposite of 4h), it means the shorter timeframe has more extreme tail events relative to noise. This 3-variant design (6:1 existing data + 7:1 new + 8:1 new) fully maps the 1h MACD R:R curve. Breakeven win rate: 11.1%.
